name: Memory ECC & Reliability
description: Sub-sector template for memory reliability patents (CPC G11C29*) covering error correction, repair, redundancy, and built-in self-test for memory.
contextDescription: Memory reliability patents cover error detection/correction and repair mechanisms. Key areas include ECC (SEC-DED, chipkill), memory BIST, redundancy repair (row/column), soft error mitigation, and endurance management. These are essential for enterprise servers, data centers, automotive, and any safety-critical application. High-value targets include server memory vendors (Samsung, SK Hynix, Micron), server processor vendors (Intel, AMD), automotive SoC vendors (NXP, Renesas, Infineon), and embedded memory IP vendors.
scoringGuidance: ECC patents are mandatory for enterprise DRAM — every server uses them.
Memory BIST patents read on all chips with embedded SRAM (virtually every SoC).
Redundancy repair patents target DRAM and NAND flash manufacturers directly.
Soft error (SER) mitigation patents are critical for automotive and aerospace.
In-field repair patents are increasingly important as process nodes shrink.

questions.question: What error correction method does this patent cover? Score by breadth: 1-3 for specialized codes, 4-6 for standard SEC-DED, 7-10 for advanced methods (chipkill, symbol ECC, adaptive ECC) or novel approaches.
questions.reasoningPrompt: Identify the ECC approach and whether it goes beyond standard implementations.

questions.question: Does this patent cover memory built-in self-test or redundancy repair? BIST is in every chip with embedded memory. Repair directly impacts yield and cost.
questions.reasoningPrompt: Assess BIST/repair coverage and its impact on manufacturing yield.

questions.question: Is this patent relevant to enterprise server or automotive memory reliability requirements? These markets have strict reliability mandates and premium pricing.
questions.reasoningPrompt: Identify enterprise or automotive reliability requirements addressed.

questions.question: Does this patent address runtime error handling (in-field, online repair) or manufacturing-time repair? Runtime solutions are harder to design around and more valuable for ongoing licensing.
questions.reasoningPrompt: Distinguish between manufacturing-time and runtime reliability techniques.
